summary: Iteration 6 (business-type selector across 7 entity types) fully verified. Backend: 31/31 new iter-6 tests pass covering CIN-required (private_limited/public_limited/opc/section_8), LLP with LLPIN (AAB-1234 and AAB1234), no-MCA types (sole_proprietorship/partnership_firm), unknown business_type → 400, PAN-vs-GSTIN cross-check for all 7 types, and /seller/me returns saved business_type + matching MCA id. Frontend: /seller/signup and /seller/upgrade both render all 7 chips with correct testIDs, hint box updates dynamically, CIN/LLPIN fields show/hide correctly per type, default chip is private_limited. E2E LLP signup with AAB-1234 → /seller/dashboard with 'Business auto-verified' badge succeeded. Pre-existing hygiene fix applied: updated _valid_business helper in test_seller.py and test_seller_payouts.py to include the now-required business_type field (otherwise all 4 payout tests and existing seller tests would 422).

minor.issue: Pre-existing flake: catalog now has 18 products (9 seeded + leftover from prior iterations' seller-created listings) causing test_list_products_returns_seeded to fail. Pre-existing from iter-5, not an iter-6 regression.
Pre-existing flake: test_duplicate_gstin_returns_409 fails when the random GSTIN collides with a previously-registered one in the long-lived test DB. Pre-existing from iter-5.

critical_code_review_comments: SellerBusiness.business_type uses Field(min_length=2) but the enforced enum is set-membership inside validate_indian_business — Pydantic will accept any string of length ≥2 and only reject at runtime. Consider a typed Literal[...] / Enum on the model so OpenAPI also documents the valid values.

BusinessFields uses a horizontal ScrollView for the chip row; on a 390px viewport the 7th chip (Section 8) is off-screen until the user scrolls. UX is fine but consider a hint that the row scrolls (e.g. fade-edge or showsHorizontalScrollIndicator).

Frontend trims+falls back to null for cin/llpin before send, but the backend SellerBusiness model accepts cin/llpin as Optional[str] without min_length, so an empty string '' would also pass Pydantic and only fail at validate_indian_business. Current FE handling is correct; just noting backend tolerance.

success_rate.backend: 97% (78/80 functional tests pass — 31/31 new iter-6 + 47/49 regression; 2 pre-existing flakes documented)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 7 chips, hint, conditional CIN/LLPIN, default chip, E2E LLP signup → dashboard, /seller/upgrade parity all pass on 390x844)

seed_data_creation: No seed data added. Tests self-register unique sellers with randomised GSTIN/PAN per run; all rows prefixed TEST_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-6 is fully green. Two long-standing hygiene flakes (products seed count and duplicate-GSTIN test) are unrelated to business-type changes and were already documented in iter-5. If the user requests stability, drop & re-seed the DB before running test_products.py, and reset the GSTIN counter / make _gstin() use a wider random alphabet in test_seller.py.
